purpose: Same-shape AMD 4xMI350 throughput-token gate closer to the local Ascend run: GBS128 with MBS1, which implies GA32 on 4 GPUs. This is the recommended GBS128 lane from amd_4x_gbs128_ga8_profile_plan_blocked_20260605.json because literal GBS128+GA8 on 4 GPUs requires MBS4 and has much higher OOM risk.

storage_policy.read: Kept Docker root as-is and routed source, outputs, HF/cache, MORI JIT, and AITER JIT under /scratch/sonle5. Root still had enough headroom for the overlay image.

image.build_note: Overlay build succeeded. AITER import smoke warned that ROCm/HIP JIT runtime reported missing jax during image check, but runtime built and loaded module_aiter_core, module_moe_sorting_opus, and module_moe_ck2stages_b16_b16_preshuffle_on_b16_silu_no_mulWeightStage2 during the run.

step_metrics.read: First step includes first-use MORI/AITER JIT compile and is not a throughput point.

comparison.read: GBS128/MBS1/GA32 sustains about 76.36 TFLOPs/GPU, close to the prior GBS64 4x no-AC lane. The larger global batch did not create a new materialized uplift; it mainly doubles tokens per optimizer step and therefore doubles step time while keeping per-GPU token/s similar.

follow_up.next_run: same-shape low-intrusion timing profile on 4xMI350 GBS128/MBS1/GA32
profile_focus: MORI AITER owner-rank routed VJP and dX return skew
